title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Browser and Node Client Systems · Inference Brief: Percent Encoding Query
summary: A Node-based partner SDK accepts tenant endpoints and must preserve URL, origin, IDN, DNS, and certificate distinctions across environments. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts percent encoding query increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch destination-resolution and origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Parse scheme, authority, host, port, path, query, and fragment and resolve relative references against the correct base. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
